sections.title: Talking to the Squire
Talking to the Squire again after starting the quest but before talking to Thurgo

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: So how are you doing getting a sword?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for Imcando dwarves to help me.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Please try and find them quickly... I am scared Sir Vyvin will find out!

sections.title: Talking to Thurgo before talking to Reldo

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you an Imcando dwarf? I need a special sword.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: I don't talk about that sort of thing anymore. I'm getting old.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll come back another time.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Hello. I am the squire to Sir Vyvin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how is life as a squire?

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Well, Sir Vyvin is a good guy to work for, however, I'm in a spot of trouble today. I've gone and lost Sir Vyvin's sword!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where you lost it?

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Well now, if I knew THAT it wouldn't be lost, now would it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, do you know the VAGUE AREA you lost it in?

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: No. I was carrying it for him all the way from where he had it stored in Lumbridge. It must have slipped from my pack during the trip, and you know what people are like these days...

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Someone will have just picked it up and kept it for themselves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, the kingdom is fairly abundant with swords...

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Yes. You can get bronze swords anywhere. But THIS isn't any old sword.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: The thing is, this sword is a family heirloom. It has been passed down through Vyvin's family for five generations! It was originally made by the Imcando dwarves, who were

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: a particularly skilled tribe of dwarven smiths. I doubt anyone could make it in the style they do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can make a new sword if you like...

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Thanks for the offer. I'd be surprised if you could though.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: The thing is, this sword is a family heirloom. It has been passed down through Vyvin's family for five generations! It was originally made by the Imcando dwarves, who were

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: a particularly skilled tribe of dwarven smiths. I doubt anyone could make it in the style they do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So would these dwarves make another one?

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: I'm not a hundred percent sure the Imcando tribe exists anymore. I should think Reldo, the palace librarian in Varrock, will know; he has done a lot of research on the races of Gielinor.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: I don't suppose you could try and track down the Imcando dwarves for me? I've got so much work to do...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll give it a go.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Thank you very much! As I say, the best place to start should be with Reldo...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll help, I know an Imcando dwarf!

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: What, really? That is wonderful to hear! Where is he then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's living on a cliff at the very south end of Asgarnia, south of Port Sarim. I'll go right over to him.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Thank you very much!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. I've got lots of mining work to do.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Oh man... I'm in such trouble...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I hope you find it soon.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Yes, me too. I'm not looking forward to telling Vyvin I've lost it. He's going to want it for the parade next week as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is he angry?

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: He doesn't know yet. I was hoping I could think of something to do before he does find out, But I find myself at a loss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wouldn't you prefer to be a squire for me?

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: No, sorry, I'm loyal to Sir Vyvin.

sections.title: Talking to Reldo

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Hello stranger.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about the Imcando dwarves?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: The Imcando dwarves, you say? Let's see...

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Ah, yes... For many hundreds of years, they were the world's most skilled smiths. They used secret smithing knowledge passed down from generation to generation.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Unfortunately, about a century ago, the once thriving group was wiped out during the barbarian invasions of that time.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: I believe a few of them survived, but with the bulk of their population destroyed, their numbers dwindled even further. If any are left now, they'll no doubt keep to themselves.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: However, I have heard tales of their love for redberry pie. Perhaps one of those could convince one to reveal their true heritage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I just go around offering random dwarves redberry pie?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Well, not totally random. Apparently some of the surviving Imcando made a home for themselves near the cliffs on the Asgarnian southern peninsula. That would be a good place to start.

sections.title: Talking to Thurgo
Talking to Thurgo without a Redberry Pie

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you an Imcando dwarf? I need a special sword.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: I don't talk about that sort of thing anymore. I'm getting old.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you make a special sword for me?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Well, you know what to do! Get me another redberry pie and I'll consider it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll come back another time.

sections.title: Talking to Thurgo with a Redberry Pie

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you an Imcando dwarf? I need a special sword.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: I don't talk about that sort of thing anymore. I'm getting old.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll come back another time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you like a redberry pie?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: I'd never say no to a redberry pie! We Imcando dwarves love them - they're GREAT!

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: By Guthix! THAT was good pie! Anyone who makes pie like THAT has got to be alright!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you make a special sword for me?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Well, after bringing me my favorite food I guess I should give it a go. What sort of sword is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need you to make a sword for one of Falador's knights. He had one which was passed down through five generations, but his squire has lost it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So we need an identical one to replace it.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: A knight's sword eh? Well, I'd need to know exactly how it looked before I could make a new one.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: All the Faladian knights used to have swords with unique designs according to their position. Could you bring me a picture or something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go and ask his squire and see if I can find one.

sections.title: Talking to Thurgo again without the Portrait

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that sword...

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Have you got a picture of the sword for me yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, not yet.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Well, come back when you do.

sections.title: Talking to the Squire after giving Thurgo a redberry Pie
Talking to the Squire again before finding the portrait

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: So how are you doing getting a sword?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't get the picture yet.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Please try and get it quickly... I am scared Sir Vyvin will find out!

sections.title: Searching the cupboard
Talking to the Squire again after finding the portrait

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: So how are you doing getting a sword?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the picture. I'll just take it to the dwarf now!

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Please hurry!

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: So how are you doing getting a sword?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found an Imcando dwarf but he needs a picture of the sword before he can make it.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: A picture eh? Hmmm.... The only one I can think of is in a small portrait of Sir Vyvin's father... Sir Vyvin keeps it in a cupboard in his room I think.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll try and get that then.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Please don't let him catch you! He MUSTN'T know what happened!

sections.title: Giving Thurgo the portrait

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that sword...

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Have you got a picture of the sword for me yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have found a picture of the sword I would like you to make.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: You'll need to get me some stuff to make this. I'll need two iron bars to make the sword, to start with. I'll also need an ore called blurite.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Blurite is useless for making actual weapons, except crossbows, but I'll need some as decoration for the hilt.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: It is a fairly rare ore. The only place I know to get it is under this cliff here, but it is guarded by a very powerful ice giant.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Most of the rocks in that cliff are pretty useless, and don't contain much of anything, but there's DEFINITELY some blurite in there.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: You'll need a little bit of mining experience to be able to find it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. I'll go and find them then.

sections.title: Creating the sword
Talking to the Squire before making the sword

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: So how are you doing getting a sword?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found a dwarf who will make the sword. I've just got to find the materials for it now!

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Please hurry!

sections.title: Talking to Thurgo without the materials

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: How are you doing finding those sword materials?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have any of them yet.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Well, I need a blurite ore and two iron bars. The only place I know to get blurite is under this cliff here, but it is guarded by a very powerful ice giant.

sections.title: Talking to Thurgo with only the iron bars

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you make that replacement sword now?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: How are you doing finding those sword materials?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have any blurite ore yet.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Better go get some then, huh? The only place I know to get it is under this cliff here, but it is guarded by a very powerful ice giant.

sections.title: Talking to Thurgo with only the blurite

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you make that replacement sword now?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: How are you doing finding those sword materials?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have two iron bars.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Better go get some then, huh?

sections.title: Giving both the iron bars and blurite to Thurgo

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you make that replacement sword now?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: How are you doing finding those sword materials?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have them right here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you very much!

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Just remember to call in with more pie some time!

sections.title: After the sword has been made

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for making that sword for me!

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: You're welcome - thanks for the pie!

sections.title: Giving the sword to the Squire

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have retrieved your sword for you.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: So can you un-equip it and hand it over to me now please?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have retrieved your sword for you.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Thank you, thank you, thank you! I was seriously worried I would have to own up to Sir Vyvin!
